descripcion: A large nonogram is projected on the wall. Players must solve it using clues hidden in the museum catalog. The completed image reveals the code for the service door.

configuracion.mecanica_principal: 7x7 nonogram grid projected on wall. Row and column clues are distributed across 4 museum catalog pages hidden in the room.
configuracion.mecanismo_barrera: 4-digit combination padlock on the service door
elementos_necesarios: 4 printed museum catalog pages with hidden clues
Service door or prop door with hasp
configuracion.distribucion_roles: 2-3 players search for catalog pages. 1-2 players work the nonogram at the projector. Remaining players cross-reference and verify.

pistas.texto: The projector flickers and highlights the first row clue on the catalog spine.
Each catalog page has a colored border. Match the border color to the nonogram row/column it belongs to.
The completed nonogram forms the number 7-3-9-2, which is the door code.

solucion.descripcion: Fill the 7x7 grid according to the number clues from the catalog pages. The filled cells form the digits 7-3-9-2.
solucion.verificacion: Entering 7392 on the padlock opens the service door with an audible click.
pasos_detallados: Find all 4 catalog pages hidden around the room (under table, inside frame backing, behind curtain, inside drawer)
Match each catalog page to its nonogram row/column using the colored border system
Solve the nonogram by filling cells according to the number sequences
Read the completed image as 4 digits: 7-3-9-2
Enter the code on the service door padlock
solucion.recompensa: Access to the service corridor (next area)

impresion.descripcion: Museum catalog page with blue border — nonogram rows 1-3 clues
Museum catalog page with red border — nonogram rows 4-5 clues
Museum catalog page with green border — nonogram columns 1-4 clues
Museum catalog page with yellow border — nonogram columns 5-7 clues

mobiliario: Curtain for hiding one catalog page
extras: Hasp and staple on service door or prop door

documentos_in_game.texto: Welcome to the Metropolitan Gallery. Our collection spans 3 floors and includes works from the Renaissance to Modern Art. This evening's exhibition features: The Golden Sunset (Floor 1), The Storm (Floor 2), The Silent Watch (Floor 3), and The Hidden Garden (Floor 1). Gallery hours: 9am-9pm. Security patrols: Every 15 minutes. Emergency exit: Service corridor, Floor 1. Staff note: Blue wing closed for renovation.
documentos_in_game.gm_notas: Each page has a different colored border and different nonogram clues embedded in the text layout.

barrera_fisica.ubicacion: Service door at end of gallery room

hilo_conductor.significado: First letter of 'Metropolitan' — the museum name. Players collect letters M-U-S-E to spell MUSE in the meta-puzzle.

control_movimiento.problema_potencial: All players crowd around the projector at once
control_movimiento.solucion_implementada: Catalog pages are distributed across the room, forcing searchers away from the projector. Nonogram solver stays at projector, searchers bring pages.
